verbatim_text: There are millions and billions of them. So the exploration space is really wide. Sometimes, even when you have these two, you can throw computation at this. If you assume access to a nice, smooth continuous reward function, you can try to optimize that and try to make discoveries happen at test time as well. And you can sort of find some reasonable solutions. And in some domains, it's possible. But in many domains, what we do see is that this reward function is either absent or very noisy. So think about doing drug discovery. The reward signal really comes from running wet lab tests. And that's not a very nice, smooth function that you can optimize. And it's typically a noisy one. So how do we solve this problem? The goal here is to train models in such a way such that they're effective algorithms in the sense that they can scale the test time adaptation infinitely. What do I mean by this?
